dependencies.relationship: afc CLI routes HTTP requests through the server_foundation API routing layer; URL paths and response envelopes must match spec 01 conventions
afc login command consumes POST /api/v1/auth/callback and GET /api/v1/auth/providers defined in oauth_and_users (spec 02); Authorization: Bearer scheme per spec 02
afc workspace create uses GET /api/v1/teams for slug-to-UUID resolution defined in teams (spec 03)
afc workspace and workspace token commands consume endpoints defined in workspaces_and_tokens (spec 04)

task_groups.title: Implement config package: initialization, parsing, resolution, and atomic writes

subtasks.title: Scaffold internal/config package with Config struct and TOML encoding
details: Create internal/config/config.go.
Define Config struct with fields HubURL, UserID, APIKey, KeyID (TOML tags: hub_url, user_id, api_key, key_id).
Implement Load(path string) (*Config, error) using github.com/BurntSushi/toml Decode.
Implement Save(path string, cfg *Config) error using atomic write pattern (os.CreateTemp + toml.NewEncoder + os.Rename).
Ensure temp file is created with mode 0600 using os.OpenFile or Chmod before writing.
Return typed errors for TOML parse failures to enable correct error message formatting.

subtasks.title: Implement config directory and file auto-creation on first run
details: Implement EnsureConfigDir(home string) error: create $HOME/.af/ with os.MkdirAll mode 0700.
Implement EnsureConfigFile(path string) error: if file absent, write empty Config with mode 0600.
On Load, if TOML parse fails, return descriptive error wrapping the toml error; do not modify file.
Handle the case where config.toml is a directory: detect via os.Stat and return descriptive error.
Integrate EnsureConfigDir and EnsureConfigFile into the root command's PersistentPreRunE hook.

subtasks.title: Implement config resolution precedence: flags > env vars > config file
details: Implement Resolve(flagVals map[string]string, cfg *Config) (*ResolvedConfig, error) in internal/config/resolve.go.
For hub_url: check --hub-url flag, then AF_HUB_URL env, then cfg.HubURL; error if all empty.
For user_id: check --user-id flag, then AF_HUB_USER_ID env, then cfg.UserID.
For api_key: check --api-key flag, then AF_HUB_API_KEY env, then cfg.APIKey.
For key_id: use cfg.KeyID only; no flag or env override.
On missing required value, return error with message naming the field and listing flag/env/config sources.
Register --hub-url, --user-id, --api-key as persistent flags on the root Cobra command.

subtasks.title: Implement atomic config write with temp file and rename
details: In Save(path string, cfg *Config): call os.CreateTemp(dir, 'config.toml.') to create temp file.
Write config via toml.NewEncoder(tmpFile).Encode(cfg); close temp file.
Call os.Chmod(tmpFile.Name(), 0600) before rename.
Call os.Rename(tmpFile.Name(), path); on error, return descriptive error (temp file may remain).
On CreateTemp failure, return descriptive error; original config unchanged.
Inject file system operations as function values or interface to enable stubbing in tests.

checks: Config package tests pass: go test ./internal/config/... -count=1 -v
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/config/...
Requirements 05-REQ-1.1, 05-REQ-1.2, 05-REQ-1.3, 05-REQ-2.1, 05-REQ-2.2, 05-REQ-2.3, 05-REQ-3.1, 05-REQ-3.2 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Implement root Cobra command, HTTP client, and error handling utilities

subtasks.title: Implement root Cobra command with version flag and persistent flags
details: Create cmd/afc/main.go with var version = '0.1.0' (overridable via -ldflags -X main.version=...).
Create internal/cmd/root.go with rootCmd cobra.Command; set Version field to version variable.
Configure Cobra's SetVersionTemplate to output 'afc version <semver>'.
Register --hub-url, --user-id, --api-key as PersistentFlags on rootCmd.
Add PersistentPreRunE to load and ensure config file on every command invocation.
Implement Makefile target 'build' that compiles to bin/afc with -ldflags version injection.

subtasks.title: Implement shared HTTP client with 30-second timeout and Bearer auth
details: Create internal/httpclient/client.go with NewClient() *http.Client; set Timeout: 30 * time.Second.
Implement DoRequest(ctx, method, url, apiKey, body) (*http.Response, error) helper.
Add Authorization: Bearer <apiKey> header to every authenticated request.
Network errors and timeout errors are returned as error values; callers print descriptive messages to stderr.
No retry logic; first failure is final.

subtasks.title: Implement error handling utilities: JSON error parsing, non-JSON fallback, flag validation
details: Create internal/apierror/apierror.go with HandleResponse(resp *http.Response) error.
On non-2xx: attempt json.Unmarshal of body into {error: string}; if success, return that message.
If JSON parse fails, return error with text 'unexpected response from server (HTTP <status>).'.
Create internal/validate/validate.go with ValidateExpires(val int) error returning exact error message.
ValidateExpires checks membership in {0, 30, 60, 90}; returns 'Error: --expires must be one of: 0, 30, 60, 90'.
Add ValidateNonEmpty(flag, val string) error for required string flag validation.

subtasks.title: Implement JSON pretty-print output helper
details: Create internal/output/output.go with PrintJSON(w io.Writer, data []byte) error.
Use json.Indent to format with 2-space indentation; write to provided writer (stdout).
All human-readable messages use fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, msg); never write them to stdout.
Ensure command RunE functions return errors (printed to stderr by Cobra's error handler) rather than writing directly to stdout.

checks: Root command and utility tests pass: go test ./internal/cmd/... ./internal/httpclient/... ./internal/apierror/... ./internal/validate/... ./internal/output/... -count=1 -v
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/... ./cmd/...
Binary builds successfully: make build; bin/afc --version outputs 'afc version 0.1.0'
Requirements 05-REQ-4.1, 05-REQ-4.2, 05-REQ-4.3, 05-REQ-5.1, 05-REQ-15.1, 05-REQ-15.2, 05-REQ-16.1, 05-REQ-16.2, 05-REQ-17.1 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Implement login command with OAuth authorization code flow

subtasks.title: Implement CSRF state generation and local callback HTTP server
details: Create internal/login/state.go with GenerateState() (string, error) using crypto/rand.Read(16 bytes) encoded as base64url.
Create internal/login/callback.go with StartCallbackServer() (*CallbackServer, error); bind net.Listen('tcp', ':0') to get OS-assigned port.
CallbackServer exposes Port int, WaitForCode(timeout time.Duration) (code, state string, err error).
Handler at /callback: validate state query param, capture code, respond HTTP 200 with HTML success page containing 'Login successful! You may close this tab.'.
Always print full authorization URL to stderr before waiting for callback.
On port binding failure, return descriptive error; caller exits before opening browser.

subtasks.title: Implement login command: provider validation, browser open, code exchange, config write
details: Create internal/cmd/login.go with loginCmd cobra.Command; flags: --provider (default 'github'), --expires (default 90).
RunE: call ValidateExpires; fetch GET /api/v1/auth/providers; validate --provider in list or exit with exact error message.
Generate CSRF state; start callback server; build authorization URL; call browser.OpenURL (error is non-fatal); print URL to stderr.
Wait up to 2 minutes via WaitForCode(2*time.Minute); timeout returns exit code 1 without writing config.
On callback: validate state match (mismatch -> exit 1 without writing config); exchange code via POST /api/v1/auth/callback with JSON payload {provider, code, redirect_uri, expires}.
On success: write hub_url, user_id (response.user.id), api_key (response.api_key.key), key_id (response.api_key.id) to config via atomic Save; exit code 0.

subtasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/auth/providers fetch and --expires flag validation for login
details: Implement fetchProviders(ctx, client, hubURL) ([]string, error) in internal/login/providers.go.
Call ValidateExpires before any network calls; return error immediately if invalid.
After fetching providers, check if --provider value is in the returned list using a linear search.
If not found, return formatted error: 'Error: unsupported provider: <value>. Available: <comma-separated list>'.
If GET /api/v1/auth/providers returns non-2xx, call HandleResponse and surface error to stderr.

checks: Login command tests pass: go test ./internal/login/... ./internal/cmd/... -count=1 -v -run TestLogin
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/login/... ./internal/cmd/...
TS-05-15 through TS-05-21, TS-05-E8 through TS-05-E12, TS-05-P3 all pass
Requirements 05-REQ-5.1 (login path), 05-REQ-6.1 through 05-REQ-6.7 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Implement keys commands: list, refresh, revoke

subtasks.title: Implement keys list command
details: Create internal/cmd/keys.go with keysCmd and keysListCmd cobra.Command.
RunE: resolve config; send GET /api/v1/keys with Bearer auth; on 2xx, call PrintJSON(os.Stdout, body); on non-2xx, call HandleResponse and write error to stderr; exit code 1.
Attach keysCmd as subcommand of rootCmd; attach keysListCmd as subcommand of keysCmd.

subtasks.title: Implement keys refresh command
details: Add keysRefreshCmd to keys.go.
RunE: resolve config; if key_id empty, print 'Error: key_id is not set. Run "afc login" first.' and return exit code 1 with zero network calls.
POST /api/v1/keys/<key_id>/refresh with Bearer auth.
On 2xx: unmarshal response to extract new id and key; update cfg.APIKey and cfg.KeyID; Save config atomically; PrintJSON(os.Stdout, body); exit code 0.
On non-2xx: call HandleResponse; print error to stderr; do not modify config; exit code 1.

subtasks.title: Implement keys revoke command with 404 handling
details: Add keysRevokeCmd to keys.go.
RunE: resolve config; if key_id empty, return error 'Error: key_id is not set. Run "afc login" first.'
DELETE /api/v1/keys/<key_id> with Bearer auth.
On 2xx: clear cfg.APIKey, cfg.KeyID, cfg.UserID; Save atomically; print 'API key revoked.' to stderr; exit code 0.
On 404: clear cfg.APIKey, cfg.KeyID, cfg.UserID; Save atomically; print 'API key not found on server. Local credentials cleared.' to stderr; exit code 0.
On other non-2xx: call HandleResponse; print error to stderr; do not modify config; exit code 1.
On Save failure after server success: print descriptive error to stderr; exit code 1 (config may retain stale values).

checks: Keys command tests pass: go test ./internal/cmd/... -count=1 -v -run TestKeys
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/cmd/...
Requirements 05-REQ-7.1, 05-REQ-8.1, 05-REQ-8.2, 05-REQ-9.1 through 05-REQ-9.4 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Implement workspace commands: create, list, get

subtasks.title: Implement workspace list and get commands
details: Create internal/cmd/workspace.go with workspaceCmd, workspaceListCmd, workspaceGetCmd cobra.Commands.
workspaceListCmd RunE: GET /api/v1/workspaces with Bearer auth; PrintJSON on 2xx; HandleResponse on non-2xx.
workspaceGetCmd: takes one positional arg (slug); GET /api/v1/workspaces/<slug>; PrintJSON on 2xx; HandleResponse on non-2xx including 404.
Register all three commands in the Cobra command tree.

subtasks.title: Implement workspace create with team slug resolution
details: Add workspaceCreateCmd with flags --git-url (required, non-empty), --slug (required, non-empty), --branch (optional), --team (optional).
Validate --git-url and --slug non-empty before any network call; return descriptive error if empty.
If --team provided: GET /api/v1/teams with Bearer auth; filter by slug field; zero matches -> 'team not found: <slug>'; multiple -> 'ambiguous team slug: <slug>'.
Build POST payload as map[string]interface{}; include git_url, slug always; include branch only if --branch provided; include team_id (UUID) only if --team resolved.
POST /api/v1/workspaces; on 2xx call PrintJSON; on non-2xx (including HTML 502) call HandleResponse which returns non-JSON fallback message.

checks: Workspace command tests pass: go test ./internal/cmd/... -count=1 -v -run TestWorkspace
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/cmd/...

task_groups.title: Implement workspace token commands: create, list, revoke

subtasks.title: Implement workspace token create command
details: Create internal/cmd/workspace_token.go with workspaceTokenCmd and workspaceTokenCreateCmd.
Flags: --workspace (required, non-empty), --label (optional), --expires (default 30, validated via ValidateExpires before any network call).
Build payload map: always include 'expires' as int; include 'label' only if --label provided.
POST /api/v1/workspaces/<slug>/tokens with Bearer auth and JSON payload.
On 2xx: PrintJSON(os.Stdout, body); do NOT write token to config; exit code 0.
On non-2xx: HandleResponse; print error to stderr; exit code 1.

subtasks.title: Implement workspace token list and revoke commands
details: workspaceTokenListCmd: --workspace flag (required, non-empty); GET /api/v1/workspaces/<slug>/tokens; PrintJSON on 2xx; HandleResponse on non-2xx.
workspaceTokenRevokeCmd: --workspace flag (required, non-empty); cobra.ExactArgs(1) for token-id positional arg.
RunE: DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/<slug>/tokens/<token-id> with Bearer auth.
On 2xx: fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, 'Token %s revoked.\n', tokenID); exit code 0.
On non-2xx: HandleResponse; print error to stderr; exit code 1.
Register workspaceTokenCmd under workspaceCmd with token subcommand tree.

No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/cmd/...

details: Run TS-05-40: confirm stdout equals json.Indent(serverResp, 2) for workspace list.
Run TS-05-41: confirm 'API key revoked.' only on stderr, not stdout.

task_groups.title: Wiring verification: end-to-end integration and stub audit

subtasks.title: Trace execution paths and verify entry point call chains
details: 05-PATH-1: trace 'afc login' -> rootCmd.PersistentPreRunE -> loginCmd.RunE -> fetchProviders -> StartCallbackServer -> WaitForCode -> POST /api/v1/auth/callback -> config.Save. Confirm no step is stubbed out.

05-PATH-1 continued: trace 'afc workspace create' -> workspaceCreateCmd.RunE -> GET /api/v1/teams -> POST /api/v1/workspaces -> PrintJSON. Confirm team_id UUID flows from teams response into workspace payload.
05-PATH-2: trace 'afc keys refresh' -> keysRefreshCmd.RunE -> POST /api/v1/keys/<key_id>/refresh -> config.Save with new api_key and key_id. Confirm both fields updated.

05-PATH-4: trace 'afc workspace token revoke' -> workspaceTokenRevokeCmd.RunE -> ExactArgs(1) validation -> DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/<slug>/tokens/<id> -> fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, 'Token %s revoked.', id).
05-PATH-5: trace 'afc keys revoke' with 404 -> keysRevokeCmd.RunE -> DELETE -> 404 branch -> cfg.APIKey='', cfg.KeyID='', cfg.UserID='' -> config.Save -> stderr message.

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation through the call chain
details: Confirm keys refresh: response body is unmarshalled, new api_key and key_id are extracted, passed into cfg fields, and cfg is passed to config.Save — no field is silently dropped.
Confirm keys revoke 404: response status code 404 is checked in keysRevokeCmd.RunE; the 404 branch clears cfg fields and calls config.Save; the non-404 branch calls HandleResponse.
Confirm login: response.user.id, response.api_key.key, response.api_key.id are extracted and stored into cfg.UserID, cfg.APIKey, cfg.KeyID respectively before config.Save is called.
Confirm http.Client returned by NewClient() is used in all DoRequest calls (no default http.Client usage without timeout).

subtasks.title: Audit for stub markers and dead code
details: Search entire codebase for panic("not implemented"), bare 'return nil' in non-trivial RunE functions, and // TODO markers: grep -rn 'panic("not implemented")\|// TODO' ./internal ./cmd
Confirm zero stub markers remain in production code paths (test helpers are exempt).
Confirm all cobra.Command.RunE fields are set (non-nil) for every registered command: login, keys list/refresh/revoke, workspace create/list/get, workspace token create/list/revoke.
Confirm PersistentPreRunE is set on rootCmd and calls EnsureConfigDir, EnsureConfigFile, and config.Load in that order.
Confirm all error returns from config.Save in login, keys refresh, and keys revoke are checked and produce exit code 1 with a message on stderr.

subtasks.title: Run all smoke tests with the compiled binary against mock servers
details: TS-05-SMOKE-1: compile bin/afc; run login then workspace create with team against mock server; verify config written, workspace JSON on stdout, both exit code 0.
TS-05-SMOKE-2: run 'afc keys refresh' with pre-populated config; verify config updated with new api_key/key_id, stdout has pretty-printed JSON, exit code 0.
TS-05-SMOKE-3: run login with reduced callback timeout and no callback; verify exit code 1, timeout error on stderr, config unchanged.
TS-05-SMOKE-4: run 'afc workspace token revoke' with mock 204; verify 'Token abc-token-id revoked.' on stderr, exit code 0.
TS-05-SMOKE-5: run 'afc keys revoke' with mock 404; verify credentials cleared in config, 'API key not found on server. Local credentials cleared.' on stderr, exit code 0.

subtasks.title: Verify cross-spec API surface compatibility
details: Confirm all API endpoint paths match specs 1-4 exactly: /api/v1/auth/providers, /api/v1/auth/callback, /api/v1/keys, /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh, /api/v1/keys/:key_id, /api/v1/teams, /api/v1/workspaces, /api/v1/workspaces/:slug, /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens, /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens/:token_id.
Confirm Authorization: Bearer header is set for all authenticated endpoints (not set for /api/v1/auth/providers and /api/v1/auth/callback).
Confirm POST /api/v1/auth/callback payload uses field names 'provider', 'code', 'redirect_uri', 'expires' matching spec 2 oauth_and_users contract.
Confirm POST /api/v1/workspaces payload uses field names 'git_url', 'slug', 'branch', 'team_id' matching spec 4 workspaces_and_tokens contract.
Confirm POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens payload uses field names 'label', 'expires' matching spec 4 contract.
